items.q: According to the inverted-U hypothesis, when is performance best?
items.correct: At an optimal moderate level of arousal.
items.why: Students assume 'more psyched up = better'. The inverted-U shows performance rises with arousal up to an optimum, then DECLINES with excess arousal (the athlete chokes). The optimum varies by skill.

Students lump all training together. Plyometrics specifically targets POWER - explosive jumps and bounds use rapid eccentric-then-concentric muscle action, training the stretch-shortening cycle for fast force production.

items.correct: Volume of air inhaled or exhaled in one normal breath at rest.
items.misconception: The maximum air your lungs can hold.
items.random_wrong: Air left in the lungs after maximum exhalation.
items.why: Students confuse tidal volume (resting breath, ~0.5L) with vital capacity (max breath, ~4-5L). Different lung volumes serve different purposes - tidal is the normal small ebb and flow.

items.q: Unfair treatment based on race is?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Discrimination. “Etiquette” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Sportsmanship. “Hooliganism” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many 'protected characteristics' does the UK Equality Act 2010 list?
items.why: The real figure is 9 characteristics. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
